title: Wells Fargo accused of not paying overtime at shortstaffed US branches
text: A Wells Fargo & Co employee filed a lawsuit on Thursday accusing the bank of depriving hundreds of its U.S. branch workers of overtime pay, as the bank faces an unprecedented campaign to unionize its workforce.

title: Is Wells Fargo (WFC) a Buy as Wall Street Analysts Look Optimistic?
text: The recommendations of Wall Street analysts are often relied on by investors when deciding whether to buy, sell, or hold a stock. Media reports about these brokerage-firm-employed (or sell-side) analysts changing their ratings often affect a stock's price.

title: FDIC-Insured Banks' Q3 Earnings Down on Higher Provisions
text: While FDIC-Insured banks' (like JPM, BAC, C & WFC) Q3 earnings get support from higher rates and decent loan demand, rising funding costs, weak asset quality, a fall in non-interest income and lower deposit balance act as major spoilsports.

title: Bank of America rises in premarket trade as big bank stocks look to end the month in positive territory
text: Bank of America Corp.'s stock BAC, +2.66% was up 0.3% in premarket trading on Thursday as the megabank looks to end the month in positive territory. As of Wednesday's close, Bank of America's shares had gained more than 19% in November, for their first positive month since July and best overall monthly gain since Oct. 2022, according to Dow Jones Market Data.

title: Lessons from a legend: Charlie Munger's most interesting trades
text: Charlie Munger is, without a doubt, one of the most famous investors in recent history. He served as the vice-chairman of Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE: BRK.A) for half a century and was described by Warren Buffett himself as his closest associate and right-hand man.

title: Wells Fargo: Value Investors Say 'Thank You', Mr. Market

Higher interest rates are a strong tailwind for banks' profitability, and Wells Fargo has shown resilience in its loan portfolio and deposit base.

title: Wells Fargo: I Started To Buy The 6.8% Yielding Preferred Shares
text: Wells Fargo & Company's earnings remain strong, with net interest income increasing by approximately 10% compared to last year. The bank only needed about 5.5% of its earnings to cover the preferred dividends. The Wells Fargo Series L preferred shares are non-callable and offer a fixed dividend, making them an attractive investment.

title: Wells Fargo & Company Declares Cash Dividends on Preferred Stock
text: SAN FRANCISCO--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Wells Fargo & Company (NYSE: WFC) today announced dividends on seven series of preferred stock. A quarterly cash dividend of $18.75 per share was declared on its 7.50% noncumulative perpetual convertible class A preferred stock, Series L, liquidation preference $1,000 per share, which is traded on the New York Stock Exchange under the symbol “WFCPrL”. The Series L dividend is payable on Dec. 15, 2023, to holders of record as of the close of business on Nov. 3.

title: US bank profits to drop but industry stable after March crisis -analysts
text: U.S. bank profits are expected to shrink in the coming months, but the industry has regained its footing after the biggest bank failures since the 2008 financial crisis, analysts said.

title: US regulators push Wells Fargo to improve financial crime monitoring - WSJ
text: U.S. regulators are pushing Wells Fargo to improve its monitoring system to detect criminal activity at the bank, the Wall Street Journal reported on Thursday, citing people with knowledge of the matter.

title: Wells Fargo (WFC) to Slash 50 IB Jobs Amid Deal Drought

title: Wells Fargo to cut less than 50 investment banker jobs - source
text: Wells Fargo has let go of less than 50 bankers from its corporate and investment banking unit as part of year-end pruning, a source familiar with the matter said on Tuesday.

title: Bank of America, JPMorgan Chase and Wells Fargo shares shrug off Moody's outlook cut to negative from stable

text: SAN FRANCISCO--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Wells Fargo & Company (NYSE: WFC) today announced it is working with Allspring Global Investments, a global asset management firm, to launch a Tribal Inclusion share class for the Allspring Government Money Market Fund, which will be offered exclusively to Wells Fargo corporate clients. title: Bank stocks mixed after Fed leaves rates unchanged, as Bank of America dips while Goldman Sachs rises

title: Wells Fargo's cash sweep feature faces SEC investigation
